title: Memory Tools
description: JSON Schema definitions for memory tools available to the ideas-brillantes assistant. Compatible with the claude-mem protocol. Provides semantic search, saving, timeline retrieval, and management of persistent memories stored in a local vector and SQLite database.

tools.name: search_memory
tools.description: Perform a semantic similarity search over stored memories and return the most relevant results.

query.description: Natural language search query used to find semantically similar memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of memory results to return. Defaults to 10.

threshold.description: Minimum cosine similarity score (0–1) a memory must reach to be included in results. Defaults to 0.5.

tools.name: save_memory
tools.description: Persist a new memory entry with optional tags and a category for future retrieval.

content.description: Text content of the memory to store (e.g. a fact, preference, past action, or note).

tags.description: Optional list of keyword tags to attach for filtering (e.g. ['work', 'project-x']).

tools.name: get_memory_timeline
tools.description: Retrieve memories ordered chronologically, optionally filtered by date range and category.

start.description: Optional start of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Only memories created at or after this timestamp are returned.

end.description: Optional end of the time range in ISO 8601 format. Only memories created at or before this timestamp are returned.

category.description: Optional category to filter the timeline results.

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Permanently delete a single memory entry by its unique ID.

id.description: Unique identifier of the memory entry to delete (returned by search_memory or get_memory_timeline).

tools.name: clear_memory
tools.description: Delete all memory entries, optionally scoped to a specific category. This action is irreversible.

category.description: Optional category to restrict deletion to. If omitted, ALL memories across all categories are deleted.
